indicators.name: bed
indicators.rationale: The bed is a significant obstacle to setting up a computer workstation, as it takes up a large portion of the space and is not suitable for prolonged sitting.

indicators.name: floor-wood
indicators.rationale: The wooden flooring is not a flat surface suitable for placing a laptop or keyboard.

indicators.name: table-merged
indicators.rationale: There is no flat surface available for setting up a computer workstation, such as a desk or table.

indicators.name: ceiling-merged
indicators.rationale: The ceiling is too low for comfortable headroom when sitting at a computer.

indicators.name: window-other
indicators.rationale: The window is too close to the bed, making it difficult to set up a computer workstation without blocking natural light.

indicators.name: bottle
indicators.rationale: The presence of a bottle on the floor suggests a casual, non-work environment.

reasoning_summary: The indoor scene is marginally adequate for computer work (solo). The presence of a bed and low ceiling obstructs the setup of a computer workstation, while the lack of a flat surface and low window height make it difficult to create a comfortable working environment. The presence of a bottle on the floor further suggests a casual, non-work environment.
